moduleDescription: Default iterators for some Nim types.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T: not char](a: openArray[T]): lent2 T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T: char](a: openArray[T]): T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a.

entries.name: mitems
entries.code: iterator mitems[T](a: var openArray[T]): var T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a so that you can modify the yielded value.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[IX, T](a: array[IX, T]): lent2 T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a.

entries.name: mitems
entries.code: iterator mitems[IX, T](a: var array[IX, T]): var T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a so that you can modify the yielded value.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T](a: set[T]): T {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each element of a. items iterates only over the elements that are really in the set (and not over the ones the set is able to hold).

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items(a: cstring): char {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a.

entries.name: mitems
entries.code:
iterator mitems(a: var cstring): var char {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a so that you can modify the yielded value.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T: enum and Ordinal](E: typedesc[T]): T
entries.description: Iterates over the values of E. See also enumutils.items for enums with holes.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T: Ordinal](s: Slice[T]): T
entries.description: Iterates over the slice s, yielding each value between s.a and s.b (inclusively).

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[T](a: openArray[T]): tuple[key: int, val: T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs.

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[T](a: var openArray[T]): tuple[key: int, val: var T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs. a[index] can be modified.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[IX, T](a: array[IX, T]): tuple[key: IX, val: T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs.

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[IX, T](a: var array[IX, T]): tuple[key: IX, val: var T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs. a[index] can be modified.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[T](a: seq[T]): tuple[key: int, val: T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs.

entries.name: mpairs
entries.code: iterator mpairs[T](a: var seq[T]): tuple[key: int, val: var T] {.inline.}
entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs. a[index] can be modified.

entries.description: Iterates over each item of a. Yields (index, a[index]) pairs.

entries.name: fields
entries.code:
iterator fields[T: tuple | object](x: T): RootObj {.magic: "Fields",
    noSideEffect, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Iterates over every field of x.
Warning: This really transforms the 'for' and unrolls the loop. The current implementation also has a bug that affects symbol binding in the loop body.

entries.name: fields
entries.description:
Iterates over every field of x and y.
Warning: This really transforms the 'for' and unrolls the loop. The current implementation also has a bug that affects symbol binding in the loop body.

entries.name: fieldPairs
entries.code:
iterator fieldPairs[T: tuple | object](x: T): tuple[key: string, val: RootObj] {.
    magic: "FieldPairs", noSideEffect, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Iterates over every field of x returning their name and value.

When you iterate over objects with different field types you have to use the compile time when instead of a runtime if to select the code you want to run for each type. To perform the comparison use the is operator. Another way to do the same without when is to leave the task of picking the appropriate code to a secondary proc which you overload for each field type and pass the value to.

Warning: This really transforms the 'for' and unrolls the loop. The current implementation also has a bug that affects symbol binding in the loop body.

entries.name: fieldPairs
entries.description:
Iterates over every field of x and y.
Warning: This really transforms the 'for' and unrolls the loop. The current implementation also has a bug that affects symbol binding in the loop body.
